title: Implement Advanced Rate Limiting with Token Bucket and Redis
statement:
Develop a rate limiting system that uses the Token Bucket algorithm and Redis as the storage backend. The system should support two types of users: administrators and regular users. Administrators should have unlimited requests, while regular users are limited to 100 requests per minute. The system should also account for burst requests and allow for configuration of the bucket capacity and refill rate. The rate limiting should be enforced at the API level, and the system should provide an endpoint to retrieve the current request count for a given user ID.
   
   The system should be implemented using Express.js and Redis, and the code should be well-organized and commented. The implementation should also include tests to verify the correctness of the rate limiting system.

   The endpoints you need to implement are:

   - GET /api/users/:id/requests - Returns the current request count for the user with the given ID.
   - POST /api/admin/request - Allows administrators to make requests without being limited.
   - POST /api/regular/request - Allows regular users to make requests, subject to the rate limiting constraints.

   You can use the provided `starterCode` as the base for your implementation.

   You will be tested with the following API test cases:

   - v_tc1: Attempt to make 5 requests to the regular user endpoint within 1 minute, verifying that the rate limiting is enforced correctly and the response body contains the expected request count.
   - v_tc2: Attempt to make 10 requests to the regular user endpoint within 2 minutes, verifying that the rate limiting is enforced correctly and the response body contains the expected request count.
   - h_tc1: Test that an administrator can make an unlimited number of requests.
   - h_tc2: Test that a regular user cannot make more than 100 requests per minute.
   - h_tc3: Test that the rate limiting system correctly handles burst requests.

   Please note that the Redis database and client library are already installed and configured for you. You can use the Redis client library to interact with the Redis database.
constraints: Must use the Token Bucket algorithm for rate limiting.
Must use Redis as the storage backend.
Must implement an endpoint to retrieve the current request count for a given user ID.
Must configure the bucket capacity and refill rate via environment variables.

endpoints.description: Returns the current request count for the user with the given ID.
Allows administrators to make requests without being limited.
Allows regular users to make requests, subject to the rate limiting constraints.
